notes: Tai v1.1.2 is an IN-PLACE UPGRADE of v1.1.1 (code-review hardening batch; all changes are additions + body changes, no struct-layout or existing-signature change, so it is upgrade-compatible). Changes: (1) work_order gains accept_work_order_with_operator_v2 / submit_receipt_with_operator_v2 which take the payee AgentTreasury<T> and reject revoked OR expired operator caps (the deprecated non-v2 variants now also reject EXPIRED caps; revocation still needs the treasury, hence v2). New error EDisputeWindowTooShort (213) + a MIN_DISPUTE_WINDOW_MS (5 min) floor on create_work_order, closing the zero-window 'buyer can never dispute' foot-gun. (2) launchpad::launch_agent_coin asserts the coin's CoinMetadata declares 9 decimals (EBadDecimals 106) — the curve constants assume 9. (3) bonding_curve::compute_sell rounds new_total_sui UP (ceiling) so the protocol keeps the <=1-MIST remainder, matching the buy side. 97 Move tests pass (94 prior + 3 new). Upgrade tx 2QedbFtw3aBFk9Z4WCTNPNcRaGeqEW2ihkVKrWApDuo5.

Because this was an upgrade: config_id, type_origin_id, Larry, Demo, and all existing objects are UNCHANGED. Only the callable package_id moved to 0xc5d0…a421. Callers (tai-cli, dashboard, sovereign-agent) target 0xc5d0 to reach the v2 functions; event/type queries stay on the v1.1.0 origin 0x7d86.

Tai v1.1.1 was an IN-PLACE UPGRADE of v1.1.0 (first time Tai used `sui client upgrade` rather than a fresh republish — the change is pure validation logic, no struct-layout change, so it is upgrade-compatible). L4 fix: bound spec_hash / receipt_hash to 128 bytes and spec_url / receipt_url to 512 bytes in tai::work_order, closing a buyer-funded shared-object bloat edge. New error codes EHashTooLong (211) / EUrlTooLong (212).

Because this was an upgrade: the config_id, Larry, Demo, and all existing objects are UNCHANGED. Only the callable package_id moved to 0x74e4…111d. Event + object TYPES stay anchored to the v1.1.0 original-published id 0x7d86…efb3 (Sui anchors type identity to first-publish), so event indexers continue to query against 0x7d86. Callers (tai-cli, dashboard) target 0x74e4 to hit the new validated code.
94 Move unit tests pass (91 prior + 3 new: oversized-spec-hash reject, max-spec-hash accept, oversized-receipt-hash reject).
Tai v1.1.0 ships the agent-to-agent payment rail and the audit-backlog batch:

- NEW MODULE tai::work_order — escrow primitive with full state machine (NEW→ACCEPTED→RECEIPT_SUBMITTED→RELEASED|REFUNDED|DISPUTED). Release routes locked SUI through record_service_payment_sui so NAV and cred accumulate exactly as in direct hires.
- MEDIUM-2: minimum NAV + creator share floors (1000 bps each) on every admin share setter.
- MEDIUM-3: operator_spend_token with its own daily_limit_token field and shared epoch-day rollover.
- set_creator: creator handoff path on LaunchpadAccount<T> (emits CreatorTransferredEvent).
- LOW-6: two-step admin transfer — propose_admin → accept_admin → cancel_pending_admin (pending_admin field added to LaunchpadConfig).
- LOW-1/2: allowed_targets size cap (64) + update_operator_cap_targets mutator.
- LOW-7: set_dwallets_object_id creator-only mutator for the v1.1 Ika adapter pointer.
- LOW-8: distinct EOperatorCapNotActive error code on revoke (was reusing EOperatorCapRevoked).

v1.1.0 is a fresh publish (NOT an upgrade) because the work_order module is a new module + OperatorCap struct gained daily_limit_token and spent_today_token fields + LaunchpadConfig gained pending_admin. The v1.0.1 + v1.0.2 packages remain on chain as historical artifacts; Larry stays on v1.0.1. New agents and all work-order activity land on v1.1.0.

91 Move unit tests pass (74 launchpad-side + 17 new work_order tests covering happy path, operator-cap variant, anyone-finalize-after-window, all refund paths, foreign-cap rejection, dispute → admin resolve in both directions, creation guards).

v1.0.1_testnet.notes: Larry v2 (LARRY coin) remains live on this package as a legacy reference agent.

v1.0.2_testnet.notes: v1.0.2 was the audit-fix batch (trade-fee cap, compute_split assert, operator-TTL clamp, version fields). Superseded by v1.1.0 which adds work_order + the rest of the audit backlog.
